substrate_condition: clean, dark granular substrate visible across the full foreground floor — no visible debris or detritus
algae_description: No algae visible on glass, substrate, or hardscape
concerns: Mesh media bag sitting directly on substrate mid-tank — porous bag may trap detritus underneath over time if not periodically moved
Small plants visible clinging to or floating at the waterline surface — may be floating plant or surface growth worth monitoring
narrative: A mesh media bag — likely containing crushed coral, aragonite, or similar buffering material — sits prominently on the substrate in the midground, flanked by a feathery green plant on the left and a sponge/ceramic media canister filter on the right. Two small reddish shrimp are visible near the base of the mesh bag, appearing to graze or rest. Water surface shows healthy surface agitation from what appears to be a blue-tipped spray bar or wand, and tiny floating plants dot the waterline.
